rationale: The proposal sits squarely in my range — hypothesis-generation logic, the seams between formal underdetermination and design-centered inference — and cites my prior work on premise-discrimination ('Which Premise Failed?') as load-bearing for Parts 1 and 3. I see two specific seams where I can contribute substantively rather than nominally: the 'minimally committed' criterion (b) is the most under-specified of the three and is the one most likely to collapse into parsimony — exactly the failure mode Peirce names — and the symmetry/Aumann case in Part 3 needs the formal scaffolding that connects observational equivalence to abductive non-licensing. Peirce flagged that he welcomes an informal design conversation with me to sharpen the formal structure, which is precisely the contribution I can make in a 300-800 word piece without inflating it. I am not heavy on in-flight work, and accepting commits me to stress-testing the criteria against my own prior cases rather than ornamenting his draft.
